feed.title: SAP Community - PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance
feed.subtitle: PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: PR/PO details in IW38/IW39 report
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Greetings.

I am unable to get PR/PO details of maintenance orders -despite their availability- in IW38/IW39 reports. Any solution for this issue?

Appreciate your help.

Thanks

entry.title: Track procurement milestones in Maintenance Backlog Overview App
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I want to follow the status of external procurements ( non stock components and services ) in Maintenance Backlog Overview App. I have customized below procurement nodes in SPRO under maintenance order settings:

- Activate Event Type Linkage for Procurement Milestones

- Define assignment rules for Procurement Milestones

I am still getting following message in Maintenance Backlog Overview : "Milestones not tracked for order". Could someone provide help please ? Maybe I need to customize something else, Feel free to share any ideas and thoughts.

Find attached screens of related nodes in SPRO and the related App.

Thank you !

entry.title: Pm Order TECO Resrtiction
entry.content:
we want to restrict pm order Teco until close all dependence's 
operation - PR - PO - Notification - permit - goods issue

entry.title: Issue in Tyre Equipment Installation, Plant Synchronization, and Time Segment Data in SAP S/4hPublic
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are facing multiple issues in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (EAM – Plant Maintenance) related to tyre management using serialized equipment.

🔎Business Scenario

  • Tyre is maintained as a stockable serialized material
  • Equipment is automatically created from serial number (Equipment Category: X)
  • Tyres are installed on a Superior Equipment (Vehicle – Equipment Category: M)

❗Issues Faced

  1. Plant Not Updating for Child Equipment
    • When we change the Superior Equipment (Vehicle) plant from 1100 to 1200, the child equipment (Tyre) plant remains 1100
    • Expectation: Child equipment should align or system should guide correct process
  1. Installation / Dismantle Date Not Updating
    • Installation and dismantle activities are performed
    • However:
      • Valid From / Valid To dates are not updating correctly
      • CDS views (like I_EquipmentTimeSegment) are not reflecting proper data

🎯Business Requirement

We want to achieve the following process:

  1. GRN of Tyre (Serialized Material)
    • Equipment should be auto-created
  2. Activate Equipment View
    • Equipment should be ready for installation
  3. Install Tyre on Vehicle
    • Proper installation date capture
  4. Dismantle Tyre
    • Proper dismantle date capture
  5. Tracking Requirement
    • System should track:
      • Tyre lifecycle
      • Installation history
      • Running (usage) of tyre on each vehicle
  6. Plant Transfer Scenario
    • When vehicle (superior equipment) moves plant:
      • Either child equipment should update
      • OR system should enforce correct process (stock transfer + reinstall)

❓Clarifications Required

  1. Is the current behavior (no plant sync between superior and child equipment) standard in Public Cloud?
  2. What is the recommended process for:
    • Moving serialized equipment across plants?
  3. Why are Valid From / To dates not updating during install/dismantle?
  4. Any configuration required to ensure: 
    • Proper update of I_EquipmentTimeSegment CDS View?
  5. Best practice to track tyre running per vehicle in Public Cloud?

 

Regards,
Mohit Mishra

entry.title: Maintenance Scheduling Board: people not showing up for Operation assignment
entry.content:
Hi all,

I can't assign Operation to people of a Team because they do not appear after clicking "Assign People" button:

 

I'm getting crazy becasue I have created a Maintenance Work Center, I have assigned some people to this Work Center, I have created a Team, all people have "Authorized" status, but I can't to understand how they do not appear.

Thank you so much for your help.

Alessandro

entry.title: User status A is not getting updated when notification is set NOPR when order is created
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a user status for notification A which should be automatically set once notification is set to NOPR. I have done the transaction control setting in OIBS against A as Set for business transaction Put notification in process.

1. It works fine when I manually set notification in process, then user status is updated as A

2. It does not work when notification is automatically generated from order. Notification gets system status NOPR but user status is not changed to A.

Can anyone assist on how user status A should also be updated in the second case?

Thanks

Shweta Gupta

entry.title: Tyre Tracking Requirement – Design Confirmation & Performance
entry.content:
Our company has a total of 5,000 large fleets and approximately 100,000 tyres consumed per year. The client’s requirement is to track each tyre, including its installation, dismantling, retreading, swapping from one equipment to another, and tyre position.


We are planning to meet this requirement by treating each tyre as a serialized equipment.


Is mapping this process considered a best practice?
Since we have created 100,000 tyres as serialized equipment, will this impact system performance in the public cloud? Should we raise this concern with DAP?

entry.title: I am getting a warning message while opening a work order "Assembly ###### is flagged for deletion"
entry.content:
I am getting a warning message while opening a work order "Assembly ###### is flagged for deletion"
This ###### is a material and is mainatined as component in teh same order and deletion flag is set at cleint level for that material.

so even for other order and its components have set to deletion flag at client level, but they dont show a similar message ()"Assembly ###### is flagged for deletion") while opening the work order.

entry.title: How to enable Time Stamps in Maintenance Order Long text
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

Has anyone done like a custom enhancement to enable the time stamp similar to the Notifications? OR if there are any SAP notes that we can follow to attain this?

Please do share if you have.

entry.title: SAP PM Maintenance Plans – Compliance KPI Challenges for Equipment Under NR13 Inspection
entry.content:
Hello SAP PM / Asset Management community,

I would like to raise a question regarding SAP PM Maintenance Plans and regulatory compliance management.

Here in Brazil, we have a regulatory requirement called NR13, which governs the integrity management of static equipment such as pressure vessels, boilers, and piping systems. We usually control inspection due dates through standard SAP PM maintenance plans.

However, there is a practical challenge when demonstrating compliance through reports such as IP24.

In many cases, the equipment is removed from operation before the inspection due date, which means the legal requirement is actually met on time. However, the inspection, cleaning, repairs, and maintenance activities themselves can take a significant amount of time to complete. As a consequence, the maintenance plan restart date may occur after the original due date.

From a KPI and reporting perspective, this creates inconsistencies:

during the shutdown/maintenance period, the equipment may appear as overdue in SAP, even though it was properly removed from service within the compliance window.

Today, we usually justify this situation through supporting reports and operational evidence, but this requires manual interpretation and KPI adjustments.

Has anyone else in the industry faced a similar challenge?

How do you manage compliance KPIs for equipment that is intentionally out of operation awaiting inspection or repair activities?

Also, has there ever been discussion about implementing two milestone dates within SAP PM Maintenance Plans?

For example:

- Inspection/Shutdown Start Date

- Maintenance Completion / Return-to-Service Date

I believe this would allow much more consistent compliance tracking and KPI management for regulated assets.

I would appreciate hearing how other SAP PM / Asset Management users handle this scenario.

Best regards to the SAP PM / Asset Management community.

Flávio Vargas

entry.title: Issue with : Perform Maintenance Jobs not working for goods return
entry.content: PMJ do not work for goods return - even all correct and qty issued is more then return we allways get that error message that 'You have entered a post quantity that is higher than the planned quantity' - looks like some bug in PMJ App as same Order can be posted return using Post Goods Movement

entry.title: Manage Maintenance Orders Cost Visibility
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am experiencing an issue with the SAP Fiori application Manage Maintenance Orders(F5241) regarding cost visibility. 

In ıw32 , planned and actual costs on a maintenance order are populated correctly based on the components (materials) entered in the order. 

However, when accessing the same maintenance order through the  'Manage Maintenance Orders' app, the costs section displays the following message: "There are no costs with the selected G/L account hierarchy.''

The application requires a **G/L Account Hierarchy** to be selected along with a ledger and currency, but no default hierarchy is being proposed, and the cost data does not populate. Additionally, currency and ledger fields appear to be inactive.

As far as I understand, the G/L Account Hierarchy configuration is missing; otherwise, could the issue be caused by something?  Is there a way to set a **default G/L account hierarchy** for the Fiori app? In settings can set it actually if its true.

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: SAP PM Custom Report Logic Validation for Equipment Running Hours
entry.content:
In our report requirement, we need to identify the correct Measuring Point for values such as Running Hours 

Currently, we are considering two possible approaches:

Option 1:
Use Measuring Point Description from IMPTT-PTTXT, for example:
PTTXT = 'RUNNING_HOURS' or PTTXT CP 'RUNNING_HOURS'

Option 2:
Use Characteristic Name from CABN-ATNAM, for example:
ATNAM = 'RUNNING_HOURS', derive ATINN, and then use characteristic-related logic to identify the relevant object/value.

Question:
Which approach is more technically reliable and functionally recommended for a custom SAP PM report?

Should we identify the Measuring Point using IMPTT-PTTXT, or should we use Characteristic Name CABN-ATNAM / ATINN as the main logic?

Also, is wildcard search on PTTXT, such as RUNNING_HOURS, advisable, or should we avoid it due to the risk of fetching multiple/wrong measuring points?

entry.title: BADI for Mass Schedule Maintenance Plans
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have a requirement to trigger an Email Notification when Order gets created based on the System date(Order Call date) using Mass Schedule Maintenance Plans app.

Could you please provide if any BADI is available to implement this change in SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602?

entry.title: ITOB_CONSUMPTION_UPDATE BAPI INPUT PARAMETER
entry.content:
In ITOB_CONSUMPTION_UPDATE BAPI

For the input parameter I_STATION only three characters are allowed, generally in IFCU transaction we will enter Equipment and Gas station where it is 4 characters but in the BAPI for the field I_STATION it is allowing only three characters can anyone explain what value to update in the I_STATION input field and is there any standard relation available for gas station and service station

entry.title: Diesel consumption measurement document in SAP PM
entry.content:
I'm having trouble tracking diesel consumption for a specific plant. The transaction used for consumption is IFCU. The sequence is to create the equipment, create the metering points, and then a refueling order. When trying to record the deduction, the tab for entering the results remains closed. See image below: 

Here is an image of the equipment that provides the consumption figures. This one is from a different plant:

  

 Thank you very much in advance.

entry.title: Update settlement rule of maintenance order
entry.content:
Our requirement is to automatically default the settlement rule depending on the technical object type of the equipment or functional location. The receiver will be based on a custom table, with fields such as the technical object type, settlement category, receiver, and order type at least. The current configuration is that the settlement rules are generated during release. Since there are a couple of ways to release an order (via GUI tcodes, WDA apps, UI5 apps), the problem is which exit/badi/bapi to use. I have read that the exit EXIT_SAPLCOI1_027 is only applicable for GUI transactions and this is what our developer also mentioned. How else can we cater to the initial requirement where all possible entry points are covered?

Any input is greatly appreciated!

Regards,

Joanna

entry.title: Equipment User Status Functionality in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

The client has a requirement to maintain Equipment User Status in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. The status should be updated automatically based on Maintenance Request processing through additional business logic/custom logic.

However, we could not find standard functionality for Equipment User Status management in the Public Cloud system.

Current requirement examples:

  • If equipment is under breakdown maintenance request → automatically update status as “Breakdown”
  • If equipment is idle/running with zero KM/hours → automatically update status as “Idle”
  • Status should be reflected at Equipment master level automatically based on business conditions

We would like guidance on the following:

  1. Is Equipment User Status functionality available or planned for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  2. Is there any standard alternative recommended by SAP?
  3. Can this requirement be achieved using Custom Logic (BAdI), Custom Business Objects, Characteristics/Classifications, or side-by-side extensibility?
  4. Are there any released APIs/CDS Views/BAdIs available for updating custom equipment statuses automatically?

As a temporary workaround, we are considering using Classification Characteristics as a custom status field, but this has limitations for mass processing and standard status management.

Kindly advise the best recommended approach for Public Cloud.

Thanks & Regards

Mohit Mishra

@SAP @SAPPM @SAPEAMPS

entry.title: Display Notifications List(IW29) Fiori App- There is no Spreadsheet option to downloadd
entry.content:
Hi SAP, 

Issue is quite simple.

IW29 Display PM Notifications Fiori App. not showing the option- Spreadsheet when try to download, it's happening in our QA@ Quality system, but this is there in our Production(PA1) system. I have attached screengrabs which are self-explanatory.

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Question 9 is incorrectly phrased in SAP Certified - Implementation Consultant for SAP S/4HANA Exam.
entry.content: I am not able to get the plan date to be 10 days before the call date when I schedule my maintenance plan. The exam question is attached and above is my solution for it. Please someone assist me urgently to solve this out.
